was the final event of six in the [2014–15 ISU Grand Prix of Figure Skating](/wiki/2014%E2%80%9315_ISU_Grand_Prix_of_Figure_Skating "2014–15 ISU Grand Prix of Figure Skating"), a senior-level international invitational competition series. It was held at the [Namihaya Dome](/wiki/Namihaya_Dome "Namihaya Dome") in [Osaka](/wiki/Osaka_Prefecture "Osaka Prefecture") on November 28–30. Medals were awarded in the disciplines of [men's singles, ladies' singles](/wiki/Single_skating "Single skating"), [pair skating](/wiki/Pair_skating "Pair skating"), and [ice dancing](/wiki/Ice_dancing "Ice dancing"). Skaters earned points toward qualifying for the [2014–15 Grand Prix Final](/wiki/2014%E2%80%9315_Grand_Prix_of_Figure_Skating_Final "2014–15 Grand Prix of Figure Skating Final").

Figure skating competition held in Osaka

The **2014 NHK Trophy** was the final event of six in the [2014–15 ISU Grand Prix of Figure Skating](/wiki/2014%E2%80%9315_ISU_Grand_Prix_of_Figure_Skating "2014–15 ISU Grand Prix of Figure Skating"), a senior-level international invitational competition series. It was held at the [Namihaya Dome](/wiki/Namihaya_Dome "Namihaya Dome") in [Osaka](/wiki/Osaka_Prefecture "Osaka Prefecture") on November 28–30. Medals were awarded in the disciplines of [men's singles, ladies' singles](/wiki/Single_skating "Single skating"), [pair skating](/wiki/Pair_skating "Pair skating"), and [ice dancing](/wiki/Ice_dancing "Ice dancing"). Skaters earned points toward qualifying for the [2014–15 Grand Prix Final](/wiki/2014%E2%80%9315_Grand_Prix_of_Figure_Skating_Final "2014–15 Grand Prix of Figure Skating Final").

### Changes to preliminary assignments

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2014_NHK_Trophy&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Changes to preliminary assignments")]

* On July 10, [Felicia Zhang](/wiki/Felicia_Zhang "Felicia Zhang") and [Nathan Bartholomay](/wiki/Nathan_Bartholomay "Nathan Bartholomay") were removed from the roster. On July 15, it was announced that [Mari Vartmann](/wiki/Mari_Vartmann "Mari Vartmann") / [Aaron van Cleave](/wiki/Aaron_van_Cleave "Aaron van Cleave") had been named as replacements.[[5]](#cite_note-pairs140715-5) On July 16, it was revealed that Zhang/Bartholomay had split up.[[6]](#cite_note-IceNetwork140716-6)
* On August 12, [Daisuke Murakami](/wiki/Daisuke_Murakami_(figure_skater) "Daisuke Murakami (figure skater)"), [Riona Kato](/wiki/Riona_Kato "Riona Kato"), and [Emi Hirai](/wiki/Emi_Hirai "Emi Hirai") / [Marien de la Asuncion](/wiki/Marien_de_la_Asuncion "Marien de la Asuncion") were added as host picks.[[7]](#cite_note-men140812-7)[[8]](#cite_note-ladies140812-8)[[9]](#cite_note-dance140812-9)
* On September 29, [Julia Antipova](/wiki/Julia_Antipova "Julia Antipova") / [Nodari Maisuradze](/wiki/Nodari_Maisuradze "Nodari Maisuradze") were removed from the roster, due to Antipova being hospitalized due to anorexia.[[10]](#cite_note-pairs140929-10)[[11]](#cite_note-RSport140914-11) On October 8, Arina Cherniavskaia / [Antonio Souza-Kordeyru](/wiki/Antonio_Souza-Kordeyru "Antonio Souza-Kordeyru") were announced as their replacements.[[12]](#cite_note-pairs141008-12)
* On October 9, [Guan Jinlin](/wiki/Guan_Jinlin "Guan Jinlin") was removed from the roster. No reason has been given.[[13]](#cite_note-men141009-13) On October 23, [Jeremy Ten](/wiki/Jeremy_Ten "Jeremy Ten") was announced as his replacement.[[14]](#cite_note-men141023-14)
* On October 14, [Nikol Gosviani](/wiki/Nikol_Gosviani "Nikol Gosviani") was removed from the roster. No reason has been given.[[15]](#cite_note-ladies141014-15) On October 30, [Elene Gedevanishvili](/wiki/Elene_Gedevanishvili "Elene Gedevanishvili") was announced as he replacement.[[16]](#cite_note-ladies141030-16)
* On October 27, [Kevin Reynolds](/wiki/Kevin_Reynolds_(figure_skater) "Kevin Reynolds (figure skater)") withdrew due to an injury.[[17]](#cite_note-men141027-17)[[18]](#cite_note-SkateCanada141027-18) On October 30, [Jorik Hendrickx](/wiki/Jorik_Hendrickx "Jorik Hendrickx") was announced as his replacement.[[19]](#cite_note-men141030-19)
* On October 29, it was reported by the German Skating Union that [Peter Liebers](/wiki/Peter_Liebers "Peter Liebers") was withdrawing.[[20]](#cite_note-DEU121029-20) He was officially removed on October 30.[[19]](#cite_note-men141030-19) On November 11, [Elladj Balde](/wiki/Elladj_Balde "Elladj Balde") was announced as his replacement.[[21]](#cite_note-men141111-21)
* On November 9, it was reported that [Adelina Sotnikova](/wiki/Adelina_Sotnikova "Adelina Sotnikova") withdrew due to a torn ankle ligament.[[22]](#cite_note-AP141109-22)[[23]](#cite_note-NBC141109-23) She was officially removed from the roster on November 11.[[24]](#cite_note-ladies141111-24) On November 17, [Anne Line Gjersem](/wiki/Anne_Line_Gjersem "Anne Line Gjersem") was announced as her replacement.[[25]](#cite_note-ladies141117-25)
* On November 24, [Jorik Hendrickx](/wiki/Jorik_Hendrickx "Jorik Hendrickx") and Arina Cherniavskaia / [Antonio Souza-Kordeyru](/wiki/Antonio_Souza-Kordeyru "Antonio Souza-Kordeyru") were removed from the roster.[[26]](#cite_note-yetagain1m-26)[[27]](#cite_note-yetagain2m-27) No replacements have been announced.
